normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_sorted_nondec (arr : Array Int) : Prop := by
  -- placeholder contract head; benchmark-facing obligations should assert the predicate directly
  trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The input slice is sorted in nondecreasing order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_some_implies_index_and_eq
  (arr : Array Int) (target : Int) (i : Nat)
  (hsorted : ∀ ⦃a b : Nat⦄, a ≤ b → b < arr.size → a < arr.size → arr[a] ≤ arr[b])
  (hret : binary_search_spec arr target = some i)
  : i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target := by
  -- contract obligation
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the search returns Some(i), then i is in-bounds and arr[i] equals target.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_none_implies_not_in_array
  (arr : Array Int) (target : Int)
  (hsorted : ∀ ⦃a b : Nat⦄, a ≤ b → b < arr.size → a < arr.size → arr[a] ≤ arr[b])
  (hret : binary_search_spec arr target = none)
  : ¬ (∃ i : Nat, i < arr.size ∧ arr[i] = target) := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the search returns None, then target does not occur anywhere in the array.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_terminates
  (arr : Array Int) (target : Int)
  : Terminates (fun _ => binary_search_impl arr target) := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The binary search loop terminates for all inputs by shrinking the interval each iteration.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_duplicates_not_unique_required
  (arr : Array Int) (target : Int)
  (hsorted : ∀ ⦃a b : Nat⦄, a ≤ b → b < arr.size → a < arr.size → arr[a] ≤ arr[b])
  (hret : binary_search_spec arr target = some 0)
  : arr.size > 0 → arr[0] = target := by
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The specification allows returning any matching index when duplicates exist (uniqueness is not required).
